title: DO NOT modify scripts/session-cost.py — flagged spec deviation for /check
body: New compute-persona-value.py imports PRICING and entry_cost from session_cost via sys.path insert. Lower blast radius (existing /wrap Phase 1 display unaffected); cleaner test boundary; round-3 narrowing to artifact-directory aggregation removed the per-row attribution need.

title: Refresh latency target: <2s light, 3-5s heavy adopter; cold first-run 30-60s with stderr warning
body: Spec target <5s only for typical adopters. Heavy adopters need mtime-pruning to hit 3-5s. Cold first-run 30-60s; surface in stderr.

title: 4-wave plan: spike close + schemas → engine + privacy → surfaces → end-to-end acceptance
body: Wave 0: spike close + data contracts (parallel). Wave 1: engine + privacy gates including safe_log + allowlist test (sequential after Wave 0). Wave 2: dashboard + /wrap-insights text (parallel). Wave 3: A0–A11 end-to-end. Privacy ships WITH engine, not deferred.

title: Telemetry counts-only; paths only in interactive confirmation + behind debug env
body: Spec's '(sources: cwd, config, scan): <path>, <path>, ...' becomes 'discovered N projects (sources: cwd:1, config:M, scan:K)' with no paths. Paths only emitted in interactive --scan-projects-root first-use confirmation prompt and behind MONSTERFLOW_DEBUG_PATHS=1 env (logs to ~/.cache/monsterflow/debug.log).

title: Interactive scan-roots confirmation flow with .monsterflow-no-scan opt-out sentinel
body: Adopter's first --scan-projects-root <dir> prompts: 'Confirm scan of these N roots? Append to scan-roots.confirmed? [y/N]'. Subsequent runs skip. Non-tty: refuse to scan. Per-project opt-out via .monsterflow-no-scan sentinel file. Spec delta required.

title: Substring pre-filter for 'Agent' + 'tool_use' tokens before json.loads (perf win)
body: Most lines (~99% in 73-dispatch fixture) are not Agent dispatches. Substring screen rejects them before paying ~30µs/line parse cost. Tokens must be quoted to avoid prose collisions.

title: run_state enum + run_state_counts aggregate — 6 keys required, sum equals runs_in_window
body: Six values: complete_value, missing_survival, missing_findings, missing_raw, malformed, cost_only. run_state_counts is required object with all 6 keys; A2 verifies sum == runs_in_window. Dashboard renders dominant state as .badge; complete_value renders silently.

title: Non-tty refusal preserves /autorun automation — never auto-confirms scan roots
body: --scan-projects-root invoked from dashboard-append.sh / non-interactive contexts: never auto-confirms. If not in scan-roots.confirmed, log '[persona-value] scan-roots not confirmed; skipping K roots' (count only) and proceed with cwd + config tiers.

title: Use null, never omit for missing rates
body: Schema types rates as ['number', 'null'] with [0,1] constraint when number. Reasons: idempotent byte-stable diffs with sort_keys=True; single dashboard branch (=== null); allowlist gate stays unambiguous.

title: Truncate last_seen to date-hour granularity — work-pattern privacy
body: Full ISO timestamp (2026-05-02T18:14:00Z) is a work-pattern leak. Round to YYYY-MM-DDTHH:00:00Z in both rankings JSONL and committed fixtures. Idempotency unaffected. Also rename to last_artifact_created_at (api) to pre-empt mtime-regression.

title: 6-flag CLI surface — verify each with --help smoke test before declaring shipped
body: Flags: --scan-projects-root <dir> (repeatable), --best-effort, --list-projects, --out PATH, --dry-run, --explain PERSONA[:GATE]. Help text carries Project Discovery cascade verbatim. Per global CLAUDE.md: verify each flag with `<tool> --help` smoke test before declaring shipped.

title: Persona-metrics-validator fires on first JSONL creation only via [ -f ] pre/post-check
body: 3 lines of bash in commands/wrap.md Phase 1c. Reruns on demand only. V3 trigger matches spec wording ('after first /wrap-insights run that produces persona-rankings.jsonl').

title: Cross-project fixture must exercise both A6 'top 3' and 'only N qualifying' branches
body: Wave 0 fixture should produce ≥3 qualifying rows for ≥1 (persona, gate) pair AND ≥1 with only 1 (so A6 '(only N qualifying)' branch is exercisable in tests).

title: mtime-pruned single-pass cost walk with substring pre-filter (stdlib only)
body: Stdlib only (no ijson). Substring screen for 'Agent' + 'tool_use' before json.loads. mtime-prune horizon = MIN(window.created_at) - 24h slack. Light adopter <2s; heavy adopter 3-5s; cold first-run 30-60s with stderr warning. Single-pass per file, dedupe on tool_use_id.

title: install.sh + dashboard-append.sh: DO NOT MODIFY (defer to onboarding spec)
body: Spec carve-out confirmed by integration. README at ~/.config/monsterflow/ is owned by future BACKLOG #2 (Onboarding) spec.

title: Per-machine salt for contributing_finding_ids[] — close rainbow-table threat
body: Generate ~/.config/monsterflow/finding-id-salt (256-bit random, chmod 600) on first run. ID = <gate-prefix>-sha256(salt || normalized_signature)[:10]. Cross-machine ID stability lost — explicitly NOT a v1 feature (drill-down is machine-local). Spec delta required.

title: commands/wrap.md Phase 1c: append after line 160, before line 162 (W1)
body: Insert new compute + render block at the existing Phase 1c append point. Pre/post existence check on rankings file gates the V3 validator. Does not regress legacy drift renderer.

title: Sidecar bundle pattern under file:// — both persona-roster.js AND persona-rankings-bundle.js
body: Dashboard avoids fetch() under file://. Both files loaded via <script src> setting window.PERSONA_ROSTER and window.__PERSONA_RANKINGS. compute-persona-value.py emits both. Spec only mentioned the roster sidecar; we extend the same pattern to the rankings JSONL.

title: Reserve forward-compat via schema_version: 1 — do NOT pre-reserve future field names
body: v1.1 (per-dispatch hash + agent_tool_use_id) bumps to schema_version: 2; v1 readers skip rows with schema_version > KNOWN_MAX. Reserved-but-null fields invite confusion.

title: safe_log() helper enforces output-side privacy with fixed SAFE_EVENTS enum
body: All stderr/stdout flows through one helper restricted to SAFE_EVENTS enum + value-pattern allowlist (persona/gate/state/sha256/int/ISO-date). Raw print() and sys.stderr.write() banned by grep test. SAFE_EVENTS: discovered_projects, malformed_artifact, missing_artifact, window_rolled, wrote_rankings, truncated_finding_ids, rejected_config_entry, rejected_symlink_escape.

title: Persona-roster.js carries: persona, gate, file_path, persona_content_hash, last_modified, deprecated + GENERATED_AT
body: Roster columns: join key (persona+gate), file_path (debug + tooltip), content_hash (lets dashboard detect roster-vs-row hash skew per e2), last_modified ('never run since edit' UX), deprecated (reserved for v1.1 tombstone), GENERATED_AT (sibling for staleness banner).

title: 11-column sortable table; insufficient-sample rows dim at 0.55; null-rate cells '—' sort to bottom
body: Columns: Persona, Gate, State (badge, silent for complete_value), Runs, Retention, Survived, Unique, Avg tok, Total tok, Last seen, Findings (collapsible details). WCAG AA verified at 0.55 opacity (4.6:1). Color bands on rate cells. Header tooltips explicitly call out retention-vs-survival semantics.

title: /wrap-insights text format: top + bottom 3 per dim × per gate, semantics note always present
body: 6 top/bottom dimension lines per gate (retention top+bot, survival top+bot, unique top+bot) + cost (top-3-cheapest only) + never-run line. '(only N qualifying)' annotation when fewer than 3 personas qualify. Trailing two-sentence note: 'retention is a compression ratio, not a true survival rate'.

title: Locked banner copy for privacy / stale-cache / empty-state
body: Privacy banner persistent (always visible). Stale-cache banner conditional (>14 days). Empty-state banner replaces privacy banner when no data (no PII to leak yet). All copy locked verbatim per ux output; matches existing dashboard idiom.

title: Path-traversal + symlink-escape hardening via shared validate_project_root()
body: Reject non-absolute, '..' after normalize, resolved path not under $HOME (configurable via MONSTERFLOW_ALLOWED_ROOTS), symlinks that escape $HOME. Applied to config tier 2 reader AND --scan-projects-root arg.

title: Dashboard tab as third top-level mode (not sub-tab under Judge)
body: Matches existing data-mode pattern at dashboard/index.html:70-71. Rankings are cross-project; Judge is per-project. Sub-tab would conflate.

title: PRICING + entry_cost imported from session_cost via sys.path insert (not duplicated)
body: compute-persona-value.py reuses session_cost's pricing table to avoid drift. sys.path insert because scripts/ is not a package.

title: Atomic writes via tmp + os.replace; sort_keys=True; round(x, 6) for floats
body: All three output files (rankings JSONL, rankings bundle JS, roster JS) write to .tmp + os.replace. JSON serialization uses sort_keys=True to avoid dict insertion-order drift. round(x, 6) before serialize.

title: DROP window_start_artifact_dir field — leaks adopter project structure
body: Field's only use was idempotency debug; (persona, gate) already identifies the row. Leak: contains adopter's project + feature + gate names. Spec delta required: drop entirely from row schema and dashboard.

title: Memory ceiling worst-case ~7 MB output, ~1 MB working — not a constraint
body: 10 projects × 5 yr × 50 personas: per (persona, gate, parent_session_uuid) row at ~200 bytes serialized × ~5,000 rows = ~1 MB working. Output ~7 MB. The constraint is single-pass parse time, not memory.

title: Dashboard 'Coverage' column derived from run_state_counts (e.g., '14/18 complete')
body: Avoids surfacing run_state as a sortable peer column (meaningless rank). Tooltip shows full breakdown.

title: Single allowlist file as both row schema and privacy gate (additionalProperties: false)
body: One file schemas/persona-rankings.allowlist.json with additionalProperties: false. A10 = jsonschema.validate(row, schema) per emitted line. No separate schema vs allowlist split — they are the same artifact.

title: Pre-commit hook for fixture allowlist as opt-in script (defense in depth)
body: scripts/install-precommit-hooks.sh wires pre-commit hook running tests/test-allowlist.sh whenever tests/fixtures/persona-attribution/** or dashboard/data/** are staged. Adopter-installable, not auto-enabled.
